title: Designing a Recommendation Engine for E-commerce with Collaborative Filtering
scenario: Design a recommendation engine for e-commerce that uses collaborative filtering to recommend products to users based on their past purchases and browsing history. The system must be able to handle large user and product datasets, provide accurate recommendations, and scale with the growth of the e-commerce platform. The output should be a Python script (as code) implementing the collaborative filtering algorithm. Constraints: Must use a data manipulation library (e.g., Pandas), must use a data storage library (e.g., NumPy), and must be under 400 lines of code. The evaluation criteria are: (1) accuracy of product recommendations (40 points), (2) efficiency of the collaborative filtering algorithm (25 points), (3) scalability of the system (20 points), (4) maintainability of code (5 points), and (5) adherence to best practices (10 points).
constraints: Must use a data manipulation library (e.g., Pandas)
Must use a data storage library (e.g., NumPy)
Must be under 400 lines of code

evaluationCriteria.description: Evaluates the accuracy of product recommendations generated by the collaborative filtering algorithm.
Checks the efficiency of the collaborative filtering algorithm in terms of computation time and memory usage.
Assesses the scalability of the system in terms of handling large user bases and product catalogs.
Evaluates the maintainability of the code in terms of readability, modularity, and testability.
Checks if the code adheres to best practices in terms of security, performance, and code organization.
